text.title: RSA Format Specs and Ad Strength
text.summary: Responsive Search Ad format specifications, character limits, and ad strength optimization factors.

list.item: Headlines: up to 15, max 30 characters each, up to 3 shown per impression
Descriptions: up to 4, max 90 characters each, up to 2 shown per impression
Display path: 2 fields, max 15 characters each (does not need to match real URL)
Final URL: the actual landing page

rule.statement: Write all 15 headlines and all 4 descriptions — partial RSAs have fewer combination options and produce lower ad strength, which correlates with higher CPC.
rule.rationale: Google's algorithm needs a large combination pool to find the best-performing sequence for each query and user context; fewer assets narrow the search space and cap performance.

list.item: Keywords included in at least 2-3 headlines
Character utilization close to the maximum
Variety of angles across headlines (benefit, proof, CTA, feature, offer)

rule.statement: Target "Excellent" ad strength; never publish an RSA with "Poor" strength — "Poor" RSAs receive less auction eligibility than "Excellent" ones for identical bids.
rule.rationale: Google's auction considers predicted CTR, which is modeled partly from ad strength; "Poor" RSAs are systematically disadvantaged in impression allocation.
